title: President Trump Will Go to a Big Dinner with Journalists
summary: President Donald Trump says he will go to the White House Correspondents' Dinner on April 25. This is a special event where the president meets with journalists. Trump has never gone to this dinner before as president. He was invited every year but did not attend. He said he did not go because he felt the press was not nice to him. Now, he has accepted the invitation and looks forward to it. The dinner started in 1921, and President Calvin Coolidge was the first president to go. Most presidents have attended, but some like Jimmy Carter and Richard Nixon did not go every year. The dinner was canceled in 2020 and 2021 because of the coronavirus. President Joe Biden went to the dinner during his time. Trump's decision to go this year is a big change.

background_read: The White House Correspondents' Dinner is an annual event where the president and journalists come together. It started in 1921 and has become a tradition. Presidents usually attend to talk with the press, but some have missed it. The dinner is a chance for fun and serious discussion about news and government. In recent years, it was canceled due to the pandemic, but it is back now. Trump's decision to go is interesting because he had avoided it before.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Donald Trump and journalists from the White House Correspondents' Association
WHAT: Trump will attend the White House Correspondents' Dinner for the first time as president
WHERE: The dinner is held in Washington, D.C.
WHY: Trump accepted the invitation after boycotting it in the past because he felt the press was unfair to him

perspectives.description: Trump sees this as an honor and a chance to mend relations with the press after past conflicts.
